Table rules: Keep the rest of the logic unchanged; the table creation code is already correct.
Insert the Table of Figures using InsertField(FieldType.FieldTOC, true) and then set TableOfFiguresLabel (or CaptionlessTableOfFiguresLabel) on the returned FieldToc object.
Range rules: Use FindReplaceOptions when calling Range.Replace to control case‑sensitivity and whole‑word behavior.
Loading rules: Avoid LoadOptions if the Aspose.Words version in the project does not expose it; rely on the default FontSettings instance instead.
Add the missing using directive for Aspose.Words.Loading to resolve LoadOptions.
Saving/Export rules: Prefer the overload of Document.Save that specifies SaveFormat.Pdf for clarity.
Set `TextRenderingMode` to control how text is rendered in the PDF.
Specify PDF/A compliance using the `Compliance` property.
Ensure the project references the Aspose.Words for .NET NuGet package (or the DLL) to provide Document, PdfSaveOptions, and PdfCompliance types.
Prefer direct instantiation of PdfSaveOptions instead of casting from SaveOptions.CreateSaveOptions.
Set PdfSaveOptions.Compliance to PdfCompliance.PdfA4Ua2 to enforce both PDF/A‑4 and PDF/UA‑2.
Attach the warning callback to the Document before calling Save.
Keep the `PdfSaveOptions.Compliance` setting to define the PDF standard (e.g., `PdfA1b`).
Optionally output collected warnings after the save operation.
Save the rendered document using the appropriate SaveFormat (Pdf in this example).
Keep the PdfSaveOptions.ExportDocumentStructure flag set to true to include PDF/UA tags.
Set ImageSaveOptions.Resolution to control pixel dimensions (e.g., 100 DPI for 600×600 pixels on a 6×6‑inch page).
Enable full font embedding in PDF via PdfSaveOptions.EmbedFullFonts = true.
Save the document in the desired output format (PDF in this example) using the appropriate SaveFormat enum.
Remove usage of the non‑existent GraphicsQualityOptions property – it is not part of the current Aspose.Words.ImageSaveOptions API.
Keep only properties that exist on ImageSaveOptions: TiffCompression, TiffBinarizationMethod, ThresholdForFloydSteinbergDithering, Resolution, PaperColor, UseAntiAliasing, UseHighQualityRendering.
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Keep image quality via `JpegQuality` and enable anti‑aliasing/high‑quality rendering for better visual results.
Remove the System.Drawing namespace and all Bitmap/Graphics usage – they are not available without adding the System.Drawing.Common package.
Use Aspose.Words.ImageSaveOptions to export a page as an image instead of manual rendering with Graphics.
Remove System.Drawing dependencies; use Aspose.Words.ImageSaveOptions to render pages to PNG.
Do not reference System.Drawing or any graphics classes; Aspose.Words can render directly to PDF, DOCX, etc.
Add the required using directives (System.Drawing, Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Saving).
Ensure the project references System.Drawing.Common if targeting .NET Core/5+ (the code itself does not need to change).
Layout rules: Call doc.UpdatePageLayout() before any rendering or saving to ensure layout is up‑to‑date.
